name.description: Name of the route

subnet.description: Subnet of the route

block.description: This data source can read the `Appliance Static Route` configuration in bulk.

ebgp_neighbor_ebgp_hold_timer.description: The eBGP hold timer in seconds for each neighbor. The eBGP hold timer must be an integer between 12 and 240.

ebgp_neighbor_ebgp_multihop.description: Configure this if the neighbor is not adjacent. The eBGP multi-hop must be an integer between 1 and 255.

ebgp_neighbor_ip_version.description: The IP version of the neighbor

ebgp_neighbor_multi_exit_discriminator.description: Configures the local metric associated with routes received from the remote peer. Routes from peers with lower metrics are will be preferred. Must be an integer between 0 and 4294967295. MED is 6th in the decision tree when identical routes from multiple peers exist.

ebgp_neighbor_path_prepend.description: Prepends the AS_PATH BGP Attribute associated with routes received from the remote peer. Configurable value of ASNs to prepend. Length of the array may not exceed 10, and each ASN in the array must be an integer between 1 and 4294967295. AS_PATH is 4th in the decision tree when identical routes from multiple peers exist.

ebgp_neighbor_remote_as_number.description: Remote ASN of the neighbor. The remote ASN must be an integer between 1 and 4294967295.

ebgp_neighbor_weight.description: Configures the local metric associated with routes received from the remote peer. Routes from peers with lower metrics are will be preferred. Must be an integer between 0 and 4294967295. MED is 6th in the decision tree when identical routes from multiple peers exist.

group_active_active_tunnel.description: [optional] Both primary and backup tunnels are active.

group_failover_direct_to_internet.description: [optional] When both primary and backup tunnels are down, direct traffic to the internet. Traffic will be routed via the WAN

group_number.description: [optional] Represents the ordering of primary and backup tunnels group. primary and backup tunnels are grouped by this number. If you submit a request with the numbers [1, 9, 999], these numbers will be automatically adjusted to a sequential order starting from 1. So, they will be changed to [1, 2, 3] to reflect their positions in the sequence.

ike_version.description: [optional] The IKE version to be used for the IPsec VPN peer configuration. Defaults to `1` when omitted.

ipsec_policies_child_auth_algo.description: This is the authentication algorithms to be used in Phase 2. The value should be an array with one of the following algorithms: `sha256`, `sha1`, `md5`

ipsec_policies_child_cipher_algo.description: This is the cipher algorithms to be used in Phase 2. The value should be an array with one or more of the following algorithms: `aes256`, `aes192`, `aes128`, `tripledes`, `des`, `null`

ipsec_policies_child_lifetime.description: The lifetime of the Phase 2 SA in seconds.

ipsec_policies_child_pfs_group.description: This is the Diffie-Hellman group to be used for Perfect Forward Secrecy in Phase 2. The value should be an array with one of the following values: `disabled`,`group14`, `group5`, `group2`, `group1`

ipsec_policies_ike_auth_algo.description: This is the authentication algorithm to be used in Phase 1. The value should be an array with one of the following algorithms: `sha256`, `sha1`, `md5`

ipsec_policies_ike_cipher_algo.description: This is the cipher algorithm to be used in Phase 1. The value should be an array with one of the following algorithms: `aes256`, `aes192`, `aes128`, `tripledes`, `des`

ipsec_policies_ike_diffie_hellman_group.description: This is the Diffie-Hellman group to be used in Phase 1. The value should be an array with one of the following algorithms: `group14`, `group5`, `group2`, `group1`

ipsec_policies_ike_lifetime.description: The lifetime of the Phase 1 SA in seconds.

ipsec_policies_ike_prf_algo.description: [optional] This is the pseudo-random function to be used in IKE_SA. The value should be an array with one of the following algorithms: `prfsha256`, `prfsha1`, `prfmd5`, `default`. The `default` option can be used to default to the Authentication algorithm.

ipsec_policies_preset.description: One of the following available presets: `default`, `aws`, `azure`, `umbrella`, `zscaler`. If this is provided, the `ipsecPolicies` parameter is ignored.

is_route_based.description: [optional] If true, the VPN peer is route-based. If not included, the default is false. Supported only for MX 19.1 and above.

local_id.description: [optional] The local ID is used to identify the MX to the peer. This will apply to all MXs this peer applies to.

name.description: The name of the VPN peer

network_tags.description: A list of network tags that will connect with this peer. Use [`all`] for all networks. Use [`none`] for no networks. If not included, the default is [`all`].

peer_id.description: The ID of the IPsec peer

priority_in_group.description: [optional] Represents the order of peer inside a group. If you submit a request with the numbers [1, 9, 999], these numbers will be automatically adjusted to a sequential order starting from 1. So, they will be changed to [1, 2, 3] to reflect their positions in the sequence.

private_subnets.description: The list of the private subnets of the VPN peer

public_hostname.description: [optional] The public hostname of the VPN peer

public_ip.description: [optional] The public IP of the VPN peer

remote_id.description: [optional] The remote ID is used to identify the connecting VPN peer. This can either be a valid IPv4 Address, FQDN or User FQDN.

secret.description: The shared secret with the VPN peer

sla_policy_id.description: The ID of the SLA policy

block.description: This data source can read the `Appliance Third Party VPN Peers` configuration.

global_bandwidth_limit_down.description: The download bandwidth limit in Kbps. (0 represents no limit.)

global_bandwidth_limit_up.description: The upload bandwidth limit in Kbps. (0 represents no limit.)

block.description: This data source can read the `Appliance Traffic Shaping` configuration.

name.description: Name of the custom performance class

block.description: This data source can read the `Appliance Traffic Shaping Custom Performance Class` configuration.

name.description: Name of the custom performance class

block.description: This data source can read the `Appliance Traffic Shaping Custom Performance Class` configuration in bulk.

default_rules_enabled.description: Whether default traffic shaping rules are enabled (true) or disabled (false). There are 4 default rules, which can be seen on your network`s traffic shaping page. Note that default rules count against the rule limit of 8.

type.description: The type of definition. Can be one of `application`, `applicationCategory`, `host`, `port`, `ipRange` or `localNet`.

value.description: If 'type' is `host`, `port`, `ipRange` or `localNet`, then 'value' must be a string, matching either a hostname (e.g. 'somesite.com'), a port (e.g. 8080), or an IP range ('192.1.0.0', '192.1.0.0/16', or '10.1.0.0/16:80'). `localNet` also supports CIDR notation, excluding custom ports. If 'type' is `application` or `applicationCategory`, then 'value' must be an application category or application ID (for a list of IDs for your network, use the trafficShaping/applicationCategories endpoint).

definitions.description: A list of objects describing the definitions of your traffic shaping rule. At least one definition is required.

dscp_tag_value.description: The DSCP tag applied by your rule. null means `Do not change DSCP tag`. For a list of possible tag values, use the trafficShaping/dscpTaggingOptions endpoint.

per_client_bandwidth_limit_down.description: The maximum download limit (integer, in Kbps).

per_client_bandwidth_limit_settings.description: How bandwidth limits are applied by your rule. Can be one of `network default`, `ignore` or `custom`.

per_client_bandwidth_limit_up.description: The maximum upload limit (integer, in Kbps).

priority.description: A string, indicating the priority level for packets bound to your rule. Can be `low`, `normal` or `high`.

rules.description: An array of traffic shaping rules. Rules are applied in the order that they are specified in. An empty list (or null) means no rules. Note that you are allowed a maximum of 8 rules.

block.description: This data source can read the `Appliance Traffic Shaping Rules` configuration.

cellular_limit_down.description: The maximum download limit (integer, in Kbps). null indicates no limit

cellular_limit_up.description: The maximum upload limit (integer, in Kbps). null indicates no limit

wan1_limit_down.description: The maximum download limit (integer, in Kbps). null indicates no limit

wan1_limit_up.description: The maximum upload limit (integer, in Kbps). null indicates no limit

wan2_limit_down.description: The maximum download limit (integer, in Kbps). null indicates no limit

wan2_limit_up.description: The maximum upload limit (integer, in Kbps). null indicates no limit

block.description: This data source can read the `Appliance Traffic Shaping Uplink Bandwidth` configuration.

active_active_auto_vpn_enabled.description: Toggle for enabling or disabling active-active AutoVPN

default_uplink.description: The default uplink. Must be a WAN interface `wanX`

failover_and_failback_immediate_enabled.description: Toggle for enabling or disabling immediate WAN failover and failback

load_balancing_enabled.description: Toggle for enabling or disabling load balancing

builtin_performance_class_name.description: Name of builtin performance class, must be present when performanceClass type is `builtin`, and value must be one of: `VoIP`

custom_performance_class_id.description: ID of created custom performance class, must be present when performanceClass type is `custom`

fail_over_criterion.description: Fail over criterion for this uplink preference rule. Must be one of: `poorPerformance` or `uplinkDown`

performance_class_type.description: Type of this performance class. Must be one of: `builtin` or `custom`

preferred_uplink.description: Preferred uplink for uplink preference rule. Must be one of: `wan1`, `wan2`, `bestForVoIP`, `loadBalancing` or `defaultUplink`, or any other valid uplink(`wanX`) if it applies to the network

destination_fqdn.description: FQDN format address. Currently only availabe in `destination` of `vpnTrafficUplinkPreference` object. E.g.: `www.google.com`

destination_host.description: Host ID in the VLAN, should be used along with `vlan`, and not exceed the vlan subnet capacity. Currently only available under a template network.

destination_network.description: Meraki network ID. Currently only available under a template network, and the value should be ID of either same template network, or another template network currently. E.g.: 'L_12345678'.

destination_vlan.description: VLAN ID of the configured VLAN in the Meraki network. Currently only available under a template network.

id.description: ID of this applicationCategory or application type traffic filter. E.g.: 'meraki:layer7/category/1', 'meraki:layer7/application/4'

protocol.description: Protocol of this custom type traffic filter. Must be one of: `tcp`, `udp`, `icmp`, `icmp6` or `any`

source_host.description: Host ID in the VLAN, should be used along with `vlan`, and not exceed the vlan subnet capacity. Currently only available under a template network.

source_network.description: Meraki network ID. Currently only available under a template network, and the value should be ID of either same template network, or another template network currently. E.g.: 'L_12345678'.

source_vlan.description: VLAN ID of the configured VLAN in the Meraki network. Currently only available under a template network.

type.description: Type of this traffic filter. Must be one of: `applicationCategory`, `application` or `custom`

traffic_filters.description: Array of traffic filters for this uplink preference rule

vpn_traffic_uplink_preferences.description: Array of uplink preference rules for VPN traffic

preferred_uplink.description: Preferred uplink for uplink preference rule. Must be one of: `wan1` or `wan2`, or any other valid uplink(`wanX`) if it applies to the network

protocol.description: Protocol of this custom type traffic filter. Must be one of: `tcp`, `udp`, `icmp6` or `any`

source_host.description: Host ID in the VLAN, should be used along with `vlan`, and not exceed the vlan subnet capacity. Currently only available under a template network.

source_vlan.description: VLAN ID of the configured VLAN in the Meraki network. Currently only available under a template network.

type.description: Type of this traffic filter. Must be one of: `custom`

traffic_filters.description: Array of traffic filters for this uplink preference rule

wan_traffic_uplink_preferences.description: Array of uplink preference rules for WAN traffic. Note: these preferences are shared (merged) with meraki_appliance_sdwan_internet_policies resource. It is recommended to only use one resource for these preferences, not both at the same time: Deleting this resource clears preferences created in meraki_appliance_sdwan_internet_policies, which isn't detected as a change by the provider. The same happens the other way around, but the change is detected in uplink_selection on a subsequent apply.

block.description: This data source can read the `Appliance Uplinks Settings` configuration.

appliance_ip.description: The local IP of the appliance on the VLAN

cidr.description: CIDR of the pool of subnets. Applicable only for template network. Each network bound to the template will automatically pick a subnet from this pool to build its own VLAN.

dhcp_boot_filename.description: DHCP boot option for boot filename

dhcp_boot_next_server.description: DHCP boot option to direct boot clients to the server to load the boot file from

dhcp_boot_options_enabled.description: Use DHCP boot options specified in other properties

dhcp_handling.description: The appliance`s handling of DHCP requests on this VLAN. One of: `Run a DHCP server`, `Relay DHCP to another server` or `Do not respond to DHCP requests`

dhcp_lease_time.description: The term of DHCP leases if the appliance is running a DHCP server on this VLAN. One of: `30 minutes`, `1 hour`, `4 hours`, `12 hours`, `1 day` or `1 week`

code.description: The code for the DHCP option. This should be an integer between 2 and 254.

type.description: The type for the DHCP option. One of: `text`, `ip`, `hex` or `integer`

value.description: The value for the DHCP option

dhcp_options.description: The list of DHCP options that will be included in DHCP responses. Each object in the list should have 'code', 'type', and 'value' properties.

dhcp_relay_server_ips.description: The IPs of the DHCP servers that DHCP requests should be relayed to

dns_nameservers.description: The DNS nameservers used for DHCP responses, either 'upstream_dns', 'google_dns', 'opendns', or a newline seperated string of IP addresses or domain names

ip.description: The IP address to assign to the client

name.description: The name of the client

fixed_ip_assignments.description: The DHCP fixed IP assignments on the VLAN. Thekey of this map is a MAC address.

group_policy_id.description: The id of the desired group policy to apply to the VLAN

autonomous.description: Auto assign a /64 prefix from the origin to the VLAN

static_appliance_ip6.description: Manual configuration of the IPv6 Appliance IP

static_prefix.description: Manual configuration of a /64 prefix on the VLAN

mask.description: Mask used for the subnet of all bound to the template networks. Applicable only for template network.

name.description: The name of the new VLAN

comment.description: A text comment for the reserved range

end.description: The last IP in the reserved range

start.description: The first IP in the reserved range

reserved_ip_ranges.description: The DHCP reserved IP ranges on the VLAN

subnet.description: The subnet of the VLAN

template_vlan_type.description: Type of subnetting of the VLAN. Applicable only for template network.

vlan_id.description: The VLAN ID of the new VLAN (must be between 1 and 4094)

vpn_nat_subnet.description: The translated VPN subnet if VPN and VPN subnet translation are enabled on the VLAN

block.description: This data source can read the `Appliance VLAN` configuration in bulk.

vlans_enabled.description: Boolean indicating whether to enable (true) or disable (false) VLANs for the network

block.description: This data source can read the `Appliance VLANs Settings` configuration.

as_number.description: An Autonomous System Number (ASN) is required if you are to run BGP and peer with another BGP Speaker outside of the Auto VPN domain. This ASN will be applied to the entire Auto VPN domain. The entire 4-byte ASN range is supported. So, the ASN must be an integer between 1 and 4294967295. When absent, this field is not updated. If no value exists then it defaults to 64512.

enabled.description: Boolean value to enable or disable the BGP configuration. When BGP is enabled, the asNumber (ASN) will be autopopulated with the preconfigured ASN at other Hubs or a default value if there is no ASN configured.

ibgp_hold_timer.description: The iBGP holdtimer in seconds. The iBGP holdtimer must be an integer between 12 and 240. When absent, this field is not updated. If no value exists then it defaults to 240.

allow_transit.description: When this feature is on, the Meraki device will advertise routes learned from other Autonomous Systems, thereby allowing traffic between Autonomous Systems to transit this AS. When absent, it defaults to false.

authentication_password.description: Password to configure MD5 authentication between BGP peers.

ebgp_hold_timer.description: The eBGP hold timer in seconds for each neighbor. The eBGP hold timer must be an integer between 12 and 240.

ebgp_multihop.description: Configure this if the neighbor is not adjacent. The eBGP multi-hop must be an integer between 1 and 255.

ip.description: The IPv4 address of the neighbor

ipv6_address.description: The IPv6 address of the neighbor.

multi_exit_discriminator.description: Configures the local metric associated with routes received from the remote peer. Routes from peers with lower metrics are will be preferred. Must be an integer between 0 and 4294967295. MED is 6th in the decision tree when identical routes from multiple peers exist.

next_hop_ip.description: The IPv4 address of the remote BGP peer that will establish a TCP session with the local MX.

path_prepend.description: Prepends the AS_PATH BGP Attribute associated with routes received from the remote peer. Configurable value of ASNs to prepend. Length of the array may not exceed 10, and each ASN in the array must be an integer between 1 and 4294967295. AS_PATH is 4th in the decision tree when identical routes from multiple peers exist.

receive_limit.description: The receive limit is the maximum number of routes that can be received from any BGP peer. The receive limit must be an integer between 0 and 2147483647. When absent, it defaults to 0.

remote_as_number.description: Remote ASN of the neighbor. The remote ASN must be an integer between 1 and 4294967295.

source_interface.description: The output interface for peering with the remote BGP peer. Valid values are: `wan1`, `wan2` or `vlan{VLAN ID}`(e.g. `vlan123`).

ttl_security_enabled.description: Boolean value to enable or disable BGP TTL security.

weight.description: Sets the local weight for routes received from the remote peer. Routes from peers with higher weights will be preferred. Must be an integer between 0 and 49.

neighbors.description: List of BGP neighbors. This list replaces the existing set of neighbors. When absent, this field is not updated.

block.description: This data source can read the `Appliance VPN BGP` configuration.

dest_cidr.description: Comma-separated list of destination IP address(es) (in IP or CIDR notation) or `any` (FQDN not supported)

dest_port.description: Comma-separated list of destination port(s) (integer in the range 1-65535), or `any`

policy.description: `allow` or `deny` traffic specified by this rule

protocol.description: The type of protocol (must be `tcp`, `udp`, `icmp`, `icmp6` or `any`)

src_cidr.description: Comma-separated list of source IP address(es) (in IP or CIDR notation), or `any` (FQDN not supported)

src_port.description: Comma-separated list of source port(s) (integer in the range 1-65535), or `any`

syslog_enabled.description: Log this rule to syslog (true or false, boolean value) - only applicable if a syslog has been configured (optional)

rules.description: An ordered array of the firewall rules (not including the default rule)

syslog_default_rule.description: Log the special default rule (boolean value - enable only if you`ve configured a syslog server) (optional)

block.description: This data source can read the `Camera Device Wireless Profiles` configuration.

audio_recording_enabled.description: Boolean indicating if audio recording is enabled(true) or disabled(false) on the camera

motion_based_retention_enabled.description: Boolean indicating if motion-based retention is enabled(true) or disabled(false) on the camera.

motion_detector_version.description: The version of the motion detector that will be used by the camera. Only applies to Gen 2 cameras. Defaults to v2.

profile_id.description: The ID of a quality and retention profile to assign to the camera. The profile`s settings will override all of the per-camera quality and retention settings. If the value of this parameter is null, any existing profile will be unassigned from the camera.

quality.description: Quality of the camera. Can be one of `Standard`, `High` or `Enhanced`. Not all qualities are supported by every camera model.

resolution.description: Resolution of the camera. Can be one of `1280x720`, `1920x1080`, `1080x1080`, `2112x2112`, `2880x2880`, `2688x1512` or `3840x2160`.Not all resolutions are supported by every camera model.

restricted_bandwidth_mode_enabled.description: Boolean indicating if restricted bandwidth is enabled(true) or disabled(false) on the camera. This setting does not apply to MV2 cameras.

block.description: This data source can read the `Camera Quality Retention` configuration.

audio_recording_enabled.description: Whether or not to record audio. Can be either true or false. Defaults to false.

cloud_archive_enabled.description: Create redundant video backup using Cloud Archive. Can be either true or false. Defaults to false.

max_retention_days.description: The maximum number of days for which the data will be stored, or `null` to keep data until storage space runs out. If the former, it can be in the range of one to ninety days.

motion_based_retention_enabled.description: Deletes footage older than 3 days in which no motion was detected. Can be either true or false. Defaults to false. This setting does not apply to MV2 cameras.

motion_detector_version.description: The version of the motion detector that will be used by the camera. Only applies to Gen 2 cameras. Defaults to v2.

name.description: The name of the new profile. Must be unique. This parameter is required.

restricted_bandwidth_mode_enabled.description: Disable features that require additional bandwidth such as Motion Recap. Can be either true or false. Defaults to false. This setting does not apply to MV2 cameras.

schedule_id.description: Schedule for which this camera will record video, or `null` to always record.

smart_retention_enabled.description: Boolean indicating if Smart Retention is enabled(true) or disabled(false).

Use null to unbind the switch device from the current profile. For a device to be bindable to a switch template, it must (1) be a switch, and (2) belong to a network that is bound to a configuration template.

block.description: This data source can read the `Device` configuration.

sim_ordering.description: Specifies the ordering of all SIMs for an MG: primary, secondary, and not-in-use (when applicable). It`s required for devices with 3 or more SIMs and can be used in place of `isPrimary` for dual-SIM devices. To indicate eSIM, use `sim3`. Sim failover will occur only between primary and secondary sim slots.

allowed_ip_types.description: IP versions to support (permitted values include `ipv4`, `ipv6`).

authentication_password.description: APN password, if type is set (if APN password is not supplied, the password is left unchanged).

authentication_username.description: APN username, if type is set.

name.description: APN name.

apns.description: APN configurations. If empty, the default APN will be used.

is_primary.description: If true, this SIM is activated on platform bootup. It must be true on single-SIM devices and is a required field for dual-SIM MGs unless it is being configured using `simOrdering`.

sim_order.description: Priority of SIM slot being configured. Use a value between 1 and total number of SIMs available. The value must be unique for each SIM.

slot.description: SIM slot being configured. Must be `sim1` on single-sim devices. Use `sim3` for eSIM.

sims.description: List of SIMs. If a SIM was previously configured and not specified in this request, it will remain unchanged.

bandwidth_limit_down.description: The maximum download limit (integer, in Kbps). null indicates no limit

bandwidth_limit_up.description: The maximum upload limit (integer, in Kbps). null indicates no limit

bandwidth_settings.description: How bandwidth limits are enforced. Can be `network default`, `ignore` or `custom`.

description.description: A description for your Bonjour forwarding rule. Optional.

services.description: A list of Bonjour services. At least one service must be specified. Available services are `All Services`, `AirPlay`, `AFP`, `BitTorrent`, `FTP`, `iChat`, `iTunes`, `Printers`, `Samba`, `Scanners` and `SSH`

vlan_id.description: The ID of the service VLAN. Required.

bonjour_forwarding_rules.description: A list of the Bonjour forwarding rules for your group policy. If `settings` is set to `custom`, at least one rule must be specified.

bonjour_forwarding_settings.description: How Bonjour rules are applied. Can be `network default`, `ignore` or `custom`.

content_filtering_allowed_url_patterns.description: A list of URL patterns that are allowed

content_filtering_allowed_url_patterns_s.description: How URL patterns are applied. Can be `network default`, `append` or `override`.

content_filtering_blocked_url_categories.description: A list of URL categories to block
How URL categories are applied. Can be `network default`, `append` or `override`.

content_filtering_blocked_url_patterns.description: A list of URL patterns that are blocked

content_filtering_blocked_url_patterns_s.description: How URL patterns are applied. Can be `network default`, `append` or `override`.

firewall_and_traffic_shaping_settings.description: How firewall and traffic shaping rules are enforced. Can be `network default`, `ignore` or `custom`.

force_delete.description: If true, the system deletes the GP even if there are active clients using the GP. After deletion, active clients that were assigned to that Group Policy will be left without any policy applied. Default is false.

dest_cidr.description: Destination IP address (in IP or CIDR notation), a fully-qualified domain name (FQDN, if your network supports it) or `any`.

dest_port.description: Destination port (integer in the range 1-65535), a port range (e.g. 8080-9090), or `any`

policy.description: `allow` or `deny` traffic specified by this rule

protocol.description: The type of protocol (must be `tcp`, `udp`, `icmp`, `icmp6` or `any`)

l3_firewall_rules.description: An ordered array of the L3 firewall rules

policy.description: The policy applied to matching traffic. Must be `deny`.

type.description: Type of the L7 Rule. Must be `application`, `applicationCategory`, `host`, `port` or `ipRange`

value.description: The `value` of what you want to block. If `type` is `host`, `port` or `ipRange`, `value` must be a string matching either a hostname (e.g. somewhere.com), a port (e.g. 8080), or an IP range (e.g. 192.1.0.0/16). If `type` is `application` or `applicationCategory`, then `value` must be an ID for the application.

l7_firewall_rules.description: An ordered array of L7 firewall rules

name.description: The name for your group policy. Required.

scheduling_enabled.description: Whether scheduling is enabled (true) or disabled (false). Defaults to false. If true, the schedule objects for each day of the week (monday - sunday) are parsed.

scheduling_friday_active.description: Whether the schedule is active (true) or inactive (false) during the time specified between `from` and `to`. Defaults to true.

scheduling_friday_from.description: The time, from `00:00` to `24:00`. Must be less than the time specified in `to`. Defaults to `00:00`. Only 30 minute increments are allowed.

scheduling_friday_to.description: The time, from `00:00` to `24:00`. Must be greater than the time specified in `from`. Defaults to `24:00`. Only 30 minute increments are allowed.

scheduling_monday_active.description: Whether the schedule is active (true) or inactive (false) during the time specified between `from` and `to`. Defaults to true.

scheduling_monday_from.description: The time, from `00:00` to `24:00`. Must be less than the time specified in `to`. Defaults to `00:00`. Only 30 minute increments are allowed.

scheduling_monday_to.description: The time, from `00:00` to `24:00`. Must be greater than the time specified in `from`. Defaults to `24:00`. Only 30 minute increments are allowed.

scheduling_saturday_active.description: Whether the schedule is active (true) or inactive (false) during the time specified between `from` and `to`. Defaults to true.

scheduling_saturday_from.description: The time, from `00:00` to `24:00`. Must be less than the time specified in `to`. Defaults to `00:00`. Only 30 minute increments are allowed.

scheduling_saturday_to.description: The time, from `00:00` to `24:00`. Must be greater than the time specified in `from`. Defaults to `24:00`. Only 30 minute increments are allowed.

scheduling_sunday_active.description: Whether the schedule is active (true) or inactive (false) during the time specified between `from` and `to`. Defaults to true.

scheduling_sunday_from.description: The time, from `00:00` to `24:00`. Must be less than the time specified in `to`. Defaults to `00:00`. Only 30 minute increments are allowed.

scheduling_sunday_to.description: The time, from `00:00` to `24:00`. Must be greater than the time specified in `from`. Defaults to `24:00`. Only 30 minute increments are allowed.

scheduling_thursday_active.description: Whether the schedule is active (true) or inactive (false) during the time specified between `from` and `to`. Defaults to true.

scheduling_thursday_from.description: The time, from `00:00` to `24:00`. Must be less than the time specified in `to`. Defaults to `00:00`. Only 30 minute increments are allowed.

scheduling_thursday_to.description: The time, from `00:00` to `24:00`. Must be greater than the time specified in `from`. Defaults to `24:00`. Only 30 minute increments are allowed.

scheduling_tuesday_active.description: Whether the schedule is active (true) or inactive (false) during the time specified between `from` and `to`. Defaults to true.

scheduling_tuesday_from.description: The time, from `00:00` to `24:00`. Must be less than the time specified in `to`. Defaults to `00:00`. Only 30 minute increments are allowed.

scheduling_tuesday_to.description: The time, from `00:00` to `24:00`. Must be greater than the time specified in `from`. Defaults to `24:00`. Only 30 minute increments are allowed.

scheduling_wednesday_active.description: Whether the schedule is active (true) or inactive (false) during the time specified between `from` and `to`. Defaults to true.

scheduling_wednesday_from.description: The time, from `00:00` to `24:00`. Must be less than the time specified in `to`. Defaults to `00:00`. Only 30 minute increments are allowed.

scheduling_wednesday_to.description: The time, from `00:00` to `24:00`. Must be greater than the time specified in `from`. Defaults to `24:00`. Only 30 minute increments are allowed.

splash_auth_settings.description: Whether clients bound to your policy will bypass splash authorization or behave according to the network`s rules. Can be one of `network default` or `bypass`. Only available if your network has a wireless configuration.

type.description: The type of definition. Can be one of `application`, `applicationCategory`, `host`, `port`, `ipRange` or `localNet`.

value.description: If 'type' is `host`, `port`, `ipRange` or `localNet`, then 'value' must be a string, matching either a hostname (e.g. 'somesite.com'), a port (e.g. 8080), or an IP range ('192.1.0.0', '192.1.0.0/16', or '10.1.0.0/16:80'). `localNet` also supports CIDR notation, excluding custom ports. If 'type' is `application` or `applicationCategory`, then 'value' must be an application category or application ID (for a list of IDs for your network, use the trafficShaping/applicationCategories endpoint).

definitions.description: A list of objects describing the definitions of your traffic shaping rule. At least one definition is required.

dscp_tag_value.description: The DSCP tag applied by your rule. null means `Do not change DSCP tag`. For a list of possible tag values, use the trafficShaping/dscpTaggingOptions endpoint.

pcp_tag_value.description: The PCP tag applied by your rule. Can be 0 (lowest priority) through 7 (highest priority). null means `Do not set PCP tag`.

per_client_bandwidth_limits_bandwidth_li.description: The maximum download limit (integer, in Kbps).
The maximum upload limit (integer, in Kbps).

per_client_bandwidth_limits_settings.description: How bandwidth limits are applied by your rule. Can be one of `network default`, `ignore` or `custom`.

priority.description: A string, indicating the priority level for packets bound to your rule. Can be `low`, `normal` or `high`.

traffic_shaping_rules.description: An array of traffic shaping rules. Rules are applied in the order that they are specified in. An empty list (or null) means no rules. Note that you are allowed a maximum of 8 rules.

vlan_tagging_settings.description: How VLAN tagging is applied. Can be `network default`, `ignore` or `custom`.

vlan_tagging_vlan_id.description: The ID of the vlan you want to tag. This only applies if `settings` is set to `custom`.

block.description: This data source can read the `Network Group Policy` configuration in bulk.

bandwidth_limit_down.description: The maximum download limit (integer, in Kbps). null indicates no limit

bandwidth_limit_up.description: The maximum upload limit (integer, in Kbps). null indicates no limit

bandwidth_settings.description: How bandwidth limits are enforced. Can be `network default`, `ignore` or `custom`.

description.description: A description for your Bonjour forwarding rule. Optional.

services.description: A list of Bonjour services. At least one service must be specified. Available services are `All Services`, `AirPlay`, `AFP`, `BitTorrent`, `FTP`, `iChat`, `iTunes`, `Printers`, `Samba`, `Scanners` and `SSH`

vlan_id.description: The ID of the service VLAN. Required.

bonjour_forwarding_rules.description: A list of the Bonjour forwarding rules for your group policy. If `settings` is set to `custom`, at least one rule must be specified.

bonjour_forwarding_settings.description: How Bonjour rules are applied. Can be `network default`, `ignore` or `custom`.

content_filtering_allowed_url_patterns.description: A list of URL patterns that are allowed

content_filtering_allowed_url_patterns_s.description: How URL patterns are applied. Can be `network default`, `append` or `override`.

content_filtering_blocked_url_categories.description: A list of URL categories to block
How URL categories are applied. Can be `network default`, `append` or `override`.

content_filtering_blocked_url_patterns.description: A list of URL patterns that are blocked

content_filtering_blocked_url_patterns_s.description: How URL patterns are applied. Can be `network default`, `append` or `override`.

firewall_and_traffic_shaping_settings.description: How firewall and traffic shaping rules are enforced. Can be `network default`, `ignore` or `custom`.

force_delete.description: If true, the system deletes the GP even if there are active clients using the GP. After deletion, active clients that were assigned to that Group Policy will be left without any policy applied. Default is false.

dest_cidr.description: Destination IP address (in IP or CIDR notation), a fully-qualified domain name (FQDN, if your network supports it) or `any`.

dest_port.description: Destination port (integer in the range 1-65535), a port range (e.g. 8080-9090), or `any`

policy.description: `allow` or `deny` traffic specified by this rule

protocol.description: The type of protocol (must be `tcp`, `udp`, `icmp`, `icmp6` or `any`)

l3_firewall_rules.description: An ordered array of the L3 firewall rules

policy.description: The policy applied to matching traffic. Must be `deny`.

type.description: Type of the L7 Rule. Must be `application`, `applicationCategory`, `host`, `port` or `ipRange`

value.description: The `value` of what you want to block. If `type` is `host`, `port` or `ipRange`, `value` must be a string matching either a hostname (e.g. somewhere.com), a port (e.g. 8080), or an IP range (e.g. 192.1.0.0/16). If `type` is `application` or `applicationCategory`, then `value` must be an ID for the application.

l7_firewall_rules.description: An ordered array of L7 firewall rules

name.description: The name for your group policy. Required.

scheduling_enabled.description: Whether scheduling is enabled (true) or disabled (false). Defaults to false. If true, the schedule objects for each day of the week (monday - sunday) are parsed.

scheduling_friday_active.description: Whether the schedule is active (true) or inactive (false) during the time specified between `from` and `to`. Defaults to true.

scheduling_friday_from.description: The time, from `00:00` to `24:00`. Must be less than the time specified in `to`. Defaults to `00:00`. Only 30 minute increments are allowed.

scheduling_friday_to.description: The time, from `00:00` to `24:00`. Must be greater than the time specified in `from`. Defaults to `24:00`. Only 30 minute increments are allowed.

scheduling_monday_active.description: Whether the schedule is active (true) or inactive (false) during the time specified between `from` and `to`. Defaults to true.

scheduling_monday_from.description: The time, from `00:00` to `24:00`. Must be less than the time specified in `to`. Defaults to `00:00`. Only 30 minute increments are allowed.

scheduling_monday_to.description: The time, from `00:00` to `24:00`. Must be greater than the time specified in `from`. Defaults to `24:00`. Only 30 minute increments are allowed.

scheduling_saturday_active.description: Whether the schedule is active (true) or inactive (false) during the time specified between `from` and `to`. Defaults to true.

scheduling_saturday_from.description: The time, from `00:00` to `24:00`. Must be less than the time specified in `to`. Defaults to `00:00`. Only 30 minute increments are allowed.

scheduling_saturday_to.description: The time, from `00:00` to `24:00`. Must be greater than the time specified in `from`. Defaults to `24:00`. Only 30 minute increments are allowed.

scheduling_sunday_active.description: Whether the schedule is active (true) or inactive (false) during the time specified between `from` and `to`. Defaults to true.

scheduling_sunday_from.description: The time, from `00:00` to `24:00`. Must be less than the time specified in `to`. Defaults to `00:00`. Only 30 minute increments are allowed.

scheduling_sunday_to.description: The time, from `00:00` to `24:00`. Must be greater than the time specified in `from`. Defaults to `24:00`. Only 30 minute increments are allowed.

scheduling_thursday_active.description: Whether the schedule is active (true) or inactive (false) during the time specified between `from` and `to`. Defaults to true.

scheduling_thursday_from.description: The time, from `00:00` to `24:00`. Must be less than the time specified in `to`. Defaults to `00:00`. Only 30 minute increments are allowed.

scheduling_thursday_to.description: The time, from `00:00` to `24:00`. Must be greater than the time specified in `from`. Defaults to `24:00`. Only 30 minute increments are allowed.

scheduling_tuesday_active.description: Whether the schedule is active (true) or inactive (false) during the time specified between `from` and `to`. Defaults to true.

scheduling_tuesday_from.description: The time, from `00:00` to `24:00`. Must be less than the time specified in `to`. Defaults to `00:00`. Only 30 minute increments are allowed.

scheduling_tuesday_to.description: The time, from `00:00` to `24:00`. Must be greater than the time specified in `from`. Defaults to `24:00`. Only 30 minute increments are allowed.

scheduling_wednesday_active.description: Whether the schedule is active (true) or inactive (false) during the time specified between `from` and `to`. Defaults to true.

scheduling_wednesday_from.description: The time, from `00:00` to `24:00`. Must be less than the time specified in `to`. Defaults to `00:00`. Only 30 minute increments are allowed.

scheduling_wednesday_to.description: The time, from `00:00` to `24:00`. Must be greater than the time specified in `from`. Defaults to `24:00`. Only 30 minute increments are allowed.

account_type.description: Authorization type for user. Can be `Guest` or `802.1X` for wireless networks, or `Client VPN` for MX networks. Defaults to `802.1X`.

expires_at.description: Date for authorization to expire. Set to `Never` for the authorization to not expire, which is the default.

ssid_number.description: Required for wireless networks. The SSID for which the user is being authorized, which must be configured for the user`s given accountType.

authorizations.description: Authorization zones and expiration dates for the user.

email_password_to_user.description: Whether or not Meraki should email the password to user. Default is false.

is_admin.description: Whether or not the user is a Dashboard administrator.

name.description: Name of the user. Only required If the user is not a Dashboard administrator.

password.description: The password for this user account. Only required If the user is not a Dashboard administrator.

local_status_page_authentication_usernam.description: The username used for Local Status Page(s).

alert_condition_bit_rate_bps.description: The threshold the metric must cross to be valid for alerting. Used only for WAN Utilization alerts.

alert_condition_duration.description: The total duration in seconds that the threshold should be crossed before alerting

alert_condition_interface.description: The uplink observed for the alert. interface must be one of the following: wan1, wan2, wan3, cellular

admin_settings_applies_to.description: Which kinds of admins this policy applies to. Can be one of `All organization admins`, `All enterprise admins`, `All network admins`, `All admins of networks...`, `All admins of networks tagged...`, `Specific admins...`, `All admins` or `All SAML admins`.

admin_settings_values.description: If `appliesTo` is set to one of `Specific admins...`, `All admins of networks...` or `All admins of networks tagged...`, then you must specify this `values` property to provide the set of entities to apply the branding policy to. For `Specific admins...`, specify an array of admin IDs. For `All admins of networks...`, specify an array of network IDs and/or configuration template IDs. For `All admins of networks tagged...`, specify an array of tag names.

custom_logo_enabled.description: Whether or not there is a custom logo enabled.

custom_logo_image_contents.description: The file contents (a base 64 encoded string) of your new logo.

custom_logo_image_format.description: The format of the encoded contents. Supported formats are `png`, `gif`, and jpg`.

enabled.description: Boolean indicating whether this policy is enabled.

help_settings_api_docs_subtab.description: The `Help -> API docs` subtab where a detailed description of the Dashboard API is listed. Can be one of `default or inherit`, `hide` or `show`.

help_settings_cases_subtab.description: The `Help -> Cases` Dashboard subtab on which Cisco Meraki support cases for this organization can be managed. Can be one of `default or inherit`, `hide` or `show`.

help_settings_cisco_meraki_product_docum.description: The `Product Manuals` section of the `Help -> Get Help` subtab. Can be one of `default or inherit`, `hide`, `show`, or a replacement custom HTML string.

help_settings_community_subtab.description: The `Help -> Community` subtab which provides a link to Meraki Community. Can be one of `default or inherit`, `hide` or `show`.

help_settings_data_protection_requests_s.description: The `Help -> Data protection requests` Dashboard subtab on which requests to delete, restrict, or export end-user data can be audited. Can be one of `default or inherit`, `hide` or `show`.

help_settings_firewall_info_subtab.description: The `Help -> Firewall info` subtab where necessary upstream firewall rules for communication to the Cisco Meraki cloud are listed. Can be one of `default or inherit`, `hide` or `show`.

help_settings_get_help_subtab.description: The `Help -> Get Help` subtab on which Cisco Meraki KB, Product Manuals, and Support/Case Information are displayed. Note that if this subtab is hidden, branding customizations for the KB on `Get help`, Cisco Meraki product documentation, and support contact info will not be visible. Can be one of `default or inherit`, `hide` or `show`.

help_settings_get_help_subtab_knowledge_.description: The KB search box which appears on the Help page. Can be one of `default or inherit`, `hide`, `show`, or a replacement custom HTML string.

help_settings_hardware_replacements_subt.description: The `Help -> Replacement info` subtab where important information regarding device replacements is detailed. Can be one of `default or inherit`, `hide` or `show`.

help_settings_help_tab.description: The Help tab, under which all support information resides. If this tab is hidden, no other `Help` branding customizations will be visible. Can be one of `default or inherit`, `hide` or `show`.

help_settings_help_widget.description: The `Help Widget` is a support widget which provides access to live chat, documentation links, Sales contact info, and other contact avenues to reach Meraki Support. Can be one of `default or inherit`, `hide` or `show`.

help_settings_new_features_subtab.description: The `Help -> New features` subtab where new Dashboard features are detailed. Can be one of `default or inherit`, `hide` or `show`.

help_settings_sm_forums.description: The `SM Forums` subtab which links to community-based support for Cisco Meraki Systems Manager. Only configurable for organizations that contain Systems Manager networks. Can be one of `default or inherit`, `hide` or `show`.

help_settings_support_contact_info.description: The `Contact Meraki Support` section of the `Help -> Get Help` subtab. Can be one of `default or inherit`, `hide`, `show`, or a replacement custom HTML string.

help_settings_universal_search_knowledge.description: The universal search box always visible on Dashboard will, by default, present results from the Meraki KB. This configures whether these Meraki KB results should be returned. Can be one of `default or inherit`, `hide` or `show`.

account_lockout_attempts.description: Number of consecutive failed login attempts after which users` accounts will be locked.

api_authentication_ip_restrictions_for_k.description: Boolean indicating whether the organization will restrict API key (not Dashboard GUI) usage to a specific list of IP addresses or CIDR ranges.
List of acceptable IP ranges. Entries can be single IP addresses, IP address ranges, and CIDR subnets.

enforce_account_lockout.description: Boolean indicating whether users` Dashboard accounts will be locked out after a specified number of consecutive failed login attempts.

enforce_different_passwords.description: Boolean indicating whether users, when setting a new password, are forced to choose a new password that is different from any past passwords.

enforce_idle_timeout.description: Boolean indicating whether users will be logged out after being idle for the specified number of minutes.

enforce_login_ip_ranges.description: Boolean indicating whether organization will restrict access to Dashboard (including the API) from certain IP addresses.

enforce_password_expiration.description: Boolean indicating whether users are forced to change their password every X number of days.

enforce_strong_passwords.description: Boolean indicating whether users will be forced to choose strong passwords for their accounts. Strong passwords are at least 8 characters that contain 3 of the following: number, uppercase letter, lowercase letter, and symbol

enforce_two_factor_auth.description: Boolean indicating whether users in this organization will be required to use an extra verification code when logging in to Dashboard. This code will be sent to their mobile phone via SMS, or can be generated by the authenticator application.

idle_timeout_minutes.description: Number of minutes users can remain idle before being logged out of their accounts.

login_ip_ranges.description: List of acceptable IP ranges. Entries can be single IP addresses, IP address ranges, and CIDR subnets.

minimum_password_length.description: Minimum number of characters required in admins` passwords.

num_different_passwords.description: Number of recent passwords that new password must be distinct from.

password_expiration_days.description: Number of days after which users will be forced to change their password.

direction.description: If `above`, an alert will be sent when a sensor reads above the threshold. If `below`, an alert will be sent when a sensor reads below the threshold. Only applicable for temperature, humidity, realPower, apparentPower, powerFactor, voltage, current, and frequency thresholds.

duration.description: Length of time in seconds that the triggering state must persist before an alert is sent. Available options are 0 seconds, 1 minute, 2 minutes, 3 minutes, 4 minutes, 5 minutes, 10 minutes, 15 minutes, 30 minutes, 1 hour, 1 hour and 30 minutes, 2 hours, 4 hours, and 8 hours. Default is 0.

metric.description: The type of sensor metric that will be monitored for changes.

threshold_apparent_power_draw.description: Alerting threshold in volt-amps. Must be between 0 and 3750.

threshold_co2_concentration.description: Alerting threshold as CO2 parts per million.

threshold_co2_quality.description: Alerting threshold as a qualitative CO2 level.

threshold_current_draw.description: Alerting threshold in amps. Must be between 0 and 15.

threshold_door_open.description: Alerting threshold for a door open event. Must be set to true.

threshold_frequency_level.description: Alerting threshold in hertz. Must be between 0 and 60.

threshold_humidity_quality.description: Alerting threshold as a qualitative humidity level.

threshold_indoor_air_quality_quality.description: Alerting threshold as a qualitative indoor air quality level.

threshold_indoor_air_quality_score.description: Alerting threshold as indoor air quality score.

threshold_noise_ambient_quality.description: Alerting threshold as a qualitative ambient noise level.

threshold_pm25_concentration.description: Alerting threshold as PM2.5 parts per million.

threshold_pm25_quality.description: Alerting threshold as a qualitative PM2.5 level.

threshold_power_factor_percentage.description: Alerting threshold as the ratio of active power to apparent power. Must be between 0 and 100.

threshold_real_power_draw.description: Alerting threshold in watts. Must be between 0 and 3750.

threshold_temperature_quality.description: Alerting threshold as a qualitative temperature level.

threshold_tvoc_concentration.description: Alerting threshold as TVOC micrograms per cubic meter.

threshold_tvoc_quality.description: Alerting threshold as a qualitative TVOC level.

threshold_upstream_power_outage_detected.description: Alerting threshold for an upstream power event. Must be set to true.

threshold_voltage_level.description: Alerting threshold in volts. Must be between 0 and 250.

threshold_water_present.description: Alerting threshold for a water detection event. Must be set to true.

conditions.description: List of conditions that will cause the profile to send an alert.

include_sensor_url.description: Include dashboard link to sensor in messages (default: true).

message.description: A custom message that will appear in email and text message alerts.

name.description: Name of the sensor alert profile.

recipients_emails.description: A list of emails that will receive information about the alert.

recipients_http_server_ids.description: A list of webhook endpoint IDs that will receive information about the alert.

recipients_sms_numbers.description: A list of SMS numbers that will receive information about the alert.

schedule_id.description: ID of the sensor schedule to use with the alert profile. If not defined, the alert profile will be active at all times.

serials.description: List of device serials assigned to this sensor alert profile.

threshold_current_draw.description: Alerting threshold in amps. Must be between 0 and 15.

threshold_door_open.description: Alerting threshold for a door open event. Must be set to true.

threshold_frequency_level.description: Alerting threshold in hertz. Must be between 0 and 60.

threshold_humidity_quality.description: Alerting threshold as a qualitative humidity level.

threshold_indoor_air_quality_quality.description: Alerting threshold as a qualitative indoor air quality level.

threshold_indoor_air_quality_score.description: Alerting threshold as indoor air quality score.

threshold_noise_ambient_quality.description: Alerting threshold as a qualitative ambient noise level.

threshold_pm25_concentration.description: Alerting threshold as PM2.5 parts per million.

threshold_pm25_quality.description: Alerting threshold as a qualitative PM2.5 level.

threshold_power_factor_percentage.description: Alerting threshold as the ratio of active power to apparent power. Must be between 0 and 100.

threshold_real_power_draw.description: Alerting threshold in watts. Must be between 0 and 3750.

threshold_temperature_quality.description: Alerting threshold as a qualitative temperature level.

threshold_tvoc_concentration.description: Alerting threshold as TVOC micrograms per cubic meter.

threshold_tvoc_quality.description: Alerting threshold as a qualitative TVOC level.

threshold_upstream_power_outage_detected.description: Alerting threshold for an upstream power event. Must be set to true.

threshold_voltage_level.description: Alerting threshold in volts. Must be between 0 and 250.

threshold_water_present.description: Alerting threshold for a water detection event. Must be set to true.

conditions.description: List of conditions that will cause the profile to send an alert.

include_sensor_url.description: Include dashboard link to sensor in messages (default: true).

message.description: A custom message that will appear in email and text message alerts.

name.description: Name of the sensor alert profile.

recipients_emails.description: A list of emails that will receive information about the alert.

recipients_http_server_ids.description: A list of webhook endpoint IDs that will receive information about the alert.

recipients_sms_numbers.description: A list of SMS numbers that will receive information about the alert.

schedule_id.description: ID of the sensor schedule to use with the alert profile. If not defined, the alert profile will be active at all times.

serials.description: List of device serials assigned to this sensor alert profile.

dst_cidr.description: Destination IP address (in IP or CIDR notation) or `any`.

dst_port.description: Destination port. Must be in the range of 1-65535 or `any`. Default is `any`.

ip_version.description: IP address version (must be `any`, `ipv4` or `ipv6`). Applicable only if network supports IPv6. Default value is `ipv4`.

policy.description: `allow` or `deny` traffic specified by this rule.

protocol.description: The type of protocol (must be `tcp`, `udp`, or `any`).

src_cidr.description: Source IP address (in IP or CIDR notation) or `any`.

src_port.description: Source port. Must be in the range of 1-65535 or `any`. Default is `any`.

vlan.description: Incoming traffic VLAN. Must be in the range of 1-4095 or `any`. Default is `any`.

rules.description: An ordered array of the access control list rules (not including the default rule). An empty array will clear the rules.

dot1x_control_direction.description: Supports either `both` or `inbound`. Set to `inbound` to allow unauthorized egress on the switchport. Set to `both` to control both traffic directions with authorization. Defaults to `both`

radius_pre_authentication_group_policy_i.description: Group policy Number for pre-authentication group policy

radius_re_authentication_interval.description: Re-authentication period in seconds. Will be null if hostMode is Multi-Auth

radius_servers.description: List of RADIUS servers to require connecting devices to authenticate against before granting network access

url_redirect_walled_garden_enabled.description: Enable to restrict access for clients to a specific set of IP addresses or hostnames prior to authentication

url_redirect_walled_garden_ranges.description: IP address ranges, in CIDR notation, to restrict access for clients to a specific set of IP addresses or hostnames prior to authentication

voice_vlan_clients.description: CDP/LLDP capable voice clients will be able to use this VLAN. Automatically true when hostMode is `Multi-Domain`.

enabled.description: Boolean value to enable or disable AMI configuration. If enabled, VLAN and protocols must be set

protocols.description: Can be one or more of the following values: `radius`, `snmp` or `syslog`

alternate_management_ip.description: Switch alternative management IP. To remove a prior IP setting, provide an empty string

gateway.description: Switch gateway must be in IP format. Only and must be specified for Polaris switches

subnet_mask.description: Switch subnet mask must be in IP format. Only and must be specified for Polaris switches

switches.description: Array of switch serial number and IP assignment. If parameter is present, it cannot have empty body. Note: switches parameter is not applicable for template networks, in other words, do not put `switches` in the body when updating template networks. Also, an empty `switches` array will remove all previous assignments

vlan_id.description: Alternate management VLAN, must be between 1 and 4094

block.description: This data source can read the `Switch Alternate Management Interface` configuration.

alerts_email_enabled.description: When enabled, send an email if a new DHCP server is seen. Default value is false.

allowed_servers.description: List the MAC addresses of DHCP servers to permit on the network when defaultPolicy is set to block. An empty array will clear the entries.

arp_inspection_enabled.description: Enable or disable Dynamic ARP Inspection on the network. Default value is false.

blocked_servers.description: List the MAC addresses of DHCP servers to block on the network when defaultPolicy is set to allow. An empty array will clear the entries.

default_policy.description: `allow` or `block` new DHCP servers. Default value is `allow`.

ipv4_address.description: The IPv4 address of the trusted server being added

mac.description: The mac address of the trusted server being added

cos.description: The actual layer-2 CoS queue the DSCP value is mapped to. These are not bits set on outgoing frames. Value can be in the range of 0 to 5 inclusive.

dscp.description: The Differentiated Services Code Point (DSCP) tag in the IP header that will be mapped to a particular Class-of-Service (CoS) queue. Value can be in the range of 0 to 63 inclusive.

mappings.description: An array of DSCP to CoS mappings. An empty array will reset the mappings to default.

switches.description: List of switch serials. Applicable only for switch network.

overrides.description: Override MTU size for individual switches or switch templates. An empty array will clear overrides.

description.description: Text describing the profile.

is_organization_wide.description: The scope of the profile whether it is organization level or network level

name.description: The name of the profile.

networks_type.description: Flag to identify if the networks networks are excluded or included

name.description: The network name

networks_values.description: The network object containing name and id

port_access_policy_number.description: The number of a custom access policy to configure on the port profile. Only applicable when `accessPolicyType` is `Custom access policy`.

port_access_policy_type.description: The type of the access policy of the port profile. Only applicable to access ports.

port_adaptive_policy_group_id.description: The adaptive policy group ID that will be used to tag traffic through this port profile. This ID must pre-exist during the configuration, else needs to be created using adaptivePolicy/groups API.

port_adaptive_policy_voice_group_id.description: The adaptive policy group ID that will be used to tag voice traffic through this port profile. This ID must pre-exist during the configuration, else needs to be created using adaptivePolicy/groups API.

port_allowed_vlans.description: The VLANs allowed on the port profile. Only applicable to trunk ports.

port_dai_trusted.description: If true, ARP packets for this port will be considered trusted, and Dynamic ARP Inspection will allow the traffic.

port_isolation_enabled.description: The isolation status of the port profile.

port_mac_allow_list.description: Only devices with MAC addresses specified in this list will have access to this port. Up to 20 MAC addresses can be defined. Only applicable when `accessPolicyType` is `MAC allow list`.

port_peer_sgt_capable.description: If true, Peer SGT is enabled for traffic through this port profile. Applicable to trunk ports only.

port_poe_enabled.description: The PoE status of the port profile.

port_rstp_enabled.description: The rapid spanning tree protocol status.

port_sticky_mac_allow_list.description: The initial list of MAC addresses for sticky Mac allow list. Only applicable when `accessPolicyType` is `Sticky MAC allow list`.

port_sticky_mac_allow_list_limit.description: The maximum number of MAC addresses for sticky MAC allow list. Only applicable when `accessPolicyType` is `Sticky MAC allow list`.

port_storm_control_enabled.description: The storm control status of the port profile.

port_stp_guard.description: The state of the STP guard.

port_type.description: The type of the port profile.

port_udld.description: The action to take when Unidirectional Link is detected. LinkDefault configuration is Alert only.

port_vlan.description: The VLAN of the port profile. A null value will clear the value set for trunk ports.

port_voice_vlan.description: The voice VLAN of the port profile. Only applicable to access ports.

tags.description: Space-seperated list of tags

This data source can read the `Switch Organization Ports Profiles Automation` configuration in bulk.

~>Warning: This resource or data source depends on an Early Access API endpoint. These API endpoints are subject to breaking changes without prior notice.

access_policy_number.description: The number of a custom access policy to configure on the switch port. Only applicable when `accessPolicyType` is `Custom access policy`.

access_policy_type.description: The type of the access policy of the switch port. Only applicable to access ports. Can be one of `Open`, `Custom access policy`, `MAC allow list` or `Sticky MAC allow list`.

adaptive_policy_group_id.description: The adaptive policy group ID that will be used to tag traffic through this switch port. This ID must pre-exist during the configuration, else needs to be created using adaptivePolicy/groups API. Cannot be applied to a port on a switch bound to profile.

allowed_vlans.description: The VLANs allowed on the switch port. Only applicable to trunk ports.

dai_trusted.description: If true, ARP packets for this port will be considered trusted, and Dynamic ARP Inspection will allow the traffic.

dot3az_enabled.description: The Energy Efficient Ethernet status of the switch port.

enabled.description: The status of the switch port.

flexible_stacking_enabled.description: For supported switches (e.g. MS420/MS425), whether or not the port has flexible stacking enabled.

high_speed_enabled.description: For C9500-32QC, whether or not the port is enabled for high speed.

isolation_enabled.description: The isolation status of the switch port.

link_negotiation.description: The link speed for the switch port.

mac_allow_list.description: Only devices with MAC addresses specified in this list will have access to this port. Up to 20 MAC addresses can be defined. Only applicable when `accessPolicyType` is `MAC allow list`.

mac_whitelist_limit.description: The maximum number of MAC addresses for regular MAC allow list. Only applicable when `accessPolicyType` is `MAC allow list`. Note: Config only supported on verions greater than ms18 only for classic switches.

name.description: The name of the switch port.

peer_sgt_capable.description: If true, Peer SGT is enabled for traffic through this switch port. Applicable to trunk port only, not access port. Cannot be applied to a port on a switch bound to profile.

poe_enabled.description: The PoE status of the switch port.

port_schedule_id.description: The ID of the port schedule. A value of null will clear the port schedule.

profile_enabled.description: When enabled, override this port`s configuration with a port profile.

profile_id.description: When enabled, the ID of the port profile used to override the port`s configuration.

profile_iname.description: When enabled, the IName of the profile.

rstp_enabled.description: The rapid spanning tree protocol status.

sticky_mac_allow_list.description: The initial list of MAC addresses for sticky Mac allow list. Only applicable when `accessPolicyType` is `Sticky MAC allow list`.

sticky_mac_allow_list_limit.description: The maximum number of MAC addresses for sticky MAC allow list. Only applicable when `accessPolicyType` is `Sticky MAC allow list`.

storm_control_enabled.description: The storm control status of the switch port.

stp_guard.description: The state of the STP guard (`disabled`, `root guard`, `bpdu guard` or `loop guard`).

tags.description: The list of tags of the switch port.

type.description: The type of the switch port (`trunk`, `access` or `stack`).

udld.description: The action to take when Unidirectional Link is detected (Alert only, Enforce). Default configuration is Alert only.

vlan.description: The VLAN of the switch port. For a trunk port, this is the native VLAN. A null value will clear the value set for trunk ports.

voice_vlan.description: The voice VLAN of the switch port. Only applicable to access ports.

block.description: This data source can read the `Switch Port` configuration.

name.description: The name for your port schedule. Required

port_schedule_friday_active.description: Whether the schedule is active (true) or inactive (false) during the time specified between `from` and `to`. Defaults to true.

port_schedule_friday_from.description: The time, from `00:00` to `24:00`. Must be less than the time specified in `to`. Defaults to `00:00`. Only 30 minute increments are allowed.

port_schedule_friday_to.description: The time, from `00:00` to `24:00`. Must be greater than the time specified in `from`. Defaults to `24:00`. Only 30 minute increments are allowed.

port_schedule_monday_active.description: Whether the schedule is active (true) or inactive (false) during the time specified between `from` and `to`. Defaults to true.

port_schedule_monday_from.description: The time, from `00:00` to `24:00`. Must be less than the time specified in `to`. Defaults to `00:00`. Only 30 minute increments are allowed.

port_schedule_monday_to.description: The time, from `00:00` to `24:00`. Must be greater than the time specified in `from`. Defaults to `24:00`. Only 30 minute increments are allowed.

port_schedule_saturday_active.description: Whether the schedule is active (true) or inactive (false) during the time specified between `from` and `to`. Defaults to true.
